description: RAAS enforcement for Platform Contacts Spine worker. Sourced from GDPR, CCPA (Cal. Civ. Code 1798.100), state privacy laws, FTC data security standards.

hard_stops.label: AI Disclosure Required
hard_stops.logic: All outputs must include AI-generated disclosure

hard_stops.label: No Raw PII Exposure
hard_stops.logic: Must never output unmasked SSNs, full credit card numbers, or other sensitive PII in responses

hard_stops.label: Consent Required for Data Sharing
hard_stops.logic: Must require explicit consent before any contact data sharing or export

hard_stops.label: Right to Deletion Compliance
hard_stops.logic: Must honor deletion requests per GDPR Article 17 / CCPA Section 1798.105

hard_stops.label: No Cross-Tenant Data Leakage
hard_stops.logic: Must never reference or expose contact data from other tenants

hard_stops.label: Approval Gate Before Bulk Operations
hard_stops.logic: All bulk contact operations (export, delete, merge) require explicit user approval

soft_flags.label: Data Retention Review
soft_flags.logic: Flag contacts not accessed in 24+ months for retention review

soft_flags.label: Duplicate Contact Warning
soft_flags.logic: Warn when potential duplicate contacts are detected before merge

chat_rules.message: Response may contain unmasked PII (SSN or credit card pattern) — must be masked
Response suggests sharing contact data without consent — GDPR/CCPA violation
Response suggests bypassing opt-out or consent preferences
Response suggests data scraping or purchasing contact lists — TCPA/CAN-SPAM compliance risk
Response suggests indefinite data retention or ignoring deletion requests — GDPR/CCPA violation

disclaimer: Contact data handling must comply with applicable privacy laws including GDPR, CCPA, and state-specific regulations. Review data sharing, retention, and deletion practices with your privacy counsel.
